claims.text: The c.1935C>A (p.D645E) variant in the GAA gene was present in 7 of the 8 patients enrolled (all except Subject A1), always on Allele 1 and typically in cis with c.1726G>A.

citations.quote: Apart from Subject A1, all carried at least one allele with the c.1935C>A (p.D645E) variant in the GAA gene.

claims.text: In Table 1, subjects A2, A3, B1, B2, B3, B4, and B5 each carry c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] on Allele 1, indicating the c.1935C>A variant is present in cis with c.1726G>A across all these patients.

Subject B4 (male, IOPD, NBS-diagnosed) is homozygous for c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] on both alleles, started ERT at age 0.1 years at 40 mg/kg Q2W, and developed high ADA titers at age 1.6 years after 1.5 years of ERT.
All patients carrying the c.1935C>A variant were confirmed CRIM-positive through western blotting or genotyping prediction.

citations.quote: All IOPD patients were confirmed CRIM-positive through western blotting, described previously, or genotyping prediction

claims.text: The three LOPD patients with the c.1935C>A variant (A2, A3) had muscle weakness noted during early adolescence or at four years of age, with no cardiac manifestations.

citations.quote: Three patients were diagnosed with LOPD (Subject A1-A3) with muscle weakness noted during early adolescence, early infancy, and four years of age, respectively. None exhibited any cardiac manifestations.

claims.text: The five IOPD patients carrying c.1935C>A (B1–B5) were diagnosed between 8 days and three months of age, primarily presenting with significant cardiac complications.

citations.quote: The remaining five were diagnosed with classic IOPD between 8 days and three months of age, primarily presenting with significant cardiac complications.
